name: Oyster Sauce Roast Pork
recipeInstructions:
1. Place each piece of pork on a cutting board and trim to about 1 inch thick.
2. Combine the remaining ingredients in a bowl and marinate the meat for at least 30 minutes, or overnight.
3. Preheat the oven to 500°F (260°C). Place a pan of boiling water at the bottom of the oven.
4. Place the seasoned pork pieces on a wire rack in a roasting pan and cook for 10 minutes.
5. Turn the meat over, brush with marinade, lower the oven temperature to 375°F (190°C), and continue cooking for another 20 minutes or until the internal temperature reaches 145°F (63°C).
6. Slice the pork thinly against the grain. Serve with rice or as an appetizer. Leftovers are great in fried rice and egg foo yong.
